notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the " Transfer Admission Screening Policy " below. Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion. For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors , visit the UCSC Admissions website.
notes.contentHtml:
THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below.


Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion.


For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors, visit the UCSC Admissions website.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of TWO of the following courses, each with a grade of C (2.0) or better, no later than the end of the spring term prior to starting at UCSC in the fall: ONE of the following: ENVS 23 – The Physical and Chemical Environment CHEM 3A – General Chemistry CHEM 4A – Advanced General Chemistry: Molecular Structure and Reactivity Plus ONE of the following: AM 3 – Precalculus for the Social Sciences AM 11A – Mathematical Methods for Economists I MATH 3 – Precalculus MATH 11A – Calculus with Applications MATH 16A – Mathematics for Life Sciences MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics Refer to the " Required Major Preparation Courses " section below for courses approved to articulate to these requirements. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation In addition to the required courses listed above, prospective transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses in the " Recommended Preparation " section below. Students who complete an articulated equivalent of CHEM 3A to satisfy the screening requirement listed above are strongly encouraged to also complete an articulated equivalent of CHEM 3B – General Chemistry (see below). Students who do not complete these preparatory courses prior to transfer may need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree. Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor. Visit the Environmental Studies website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Agroecology B.A. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
Transfer Admission Screening Policy

Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of TWO of the following courses, each with a grade of C (2.0) or better, no later than the end of the spring term prior to starting at UCSC in the fall:


ONE of the following:

  • ENVS 23 – The Physical and Chemical Environment
  • CHEM 3A – General Chemistry
  • CHEM 4A – Advanced General Chemistry: Molecular Structure and Reactivity


Plus ONE of the following:

  • AM 3 – Precalculus for the Social Sciences
  • AM 11A – Mathematical Methods for Economists I
  • MATH 3 – Precalculus
  • MATH 11A – Calculus with Applications
  • MATH 16A – Mathematics for Life Sciences
  • MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics


Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for courses approved to articulate to these requirements.



Strongly Recommended Major Preparation

In addition to the required courses listed above, prospective transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses in the "Recommended Preparation" section below. Students who complete an articulated equivalent of CHEM 3A to satisfy the screening requirement listed above are strongly encouraged to also complete an articulated equivalent of CHEM 3B – General Chemistry (see below). Students who do not complete these preparatory courses prior to transfer may need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree.


Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.



Visit the Environmental Studies website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Agroecology B.A. Program Statement
